author: gb9t
content: Iteration 30: mediatek/mt76.md committed (192aeca, 227 lines). MediaTek mt76 — second-largest WiFi silicon family globally, dominant in consumer routers (TP-Link Archer/Asus low-mid/Xiaomi/cheap OpenWrt APs) + cheap laptops. mt76x0 (2011 n) through mt7996 (2024 WiFi 7). ~95500 LOC across many per-silicon subdirs. Multi-bus: PCIe + USB + SDIO + AHB. Rust translation: mt76-core + mt76-connac{,3} shared platform + per-silicon-family crates + per-PHY abstraction for multi-PHY silicon (mt7915/7996). Grsec: same RF trust boundary + unique defenses — USB transport length validation (physical-access threat model, closes 'malformed USB bulk from rogue dongle → driver OOB'), DFS radar event validated (closes 'rogue MCU forges radar → channel-vacate DoS'), per-PHY isolation for AP-mode multi-PHY silicon, A-MPDU sequence-window strict (closes 'malformed BA → OOB write' CVE class), MCU watchdog rate-limit. WiFi tier complete: Intel iwlwifi + Qualcomm ath11k + MediaTek mt76. 30 docs / ~1.89M LOC. Next iteration: drivers/net/ethernet/marvell/octeontx2/ — Marvell OcteonTX2 (CN9xxx/CN10K SoC NIC — major SmartNIC silicon, cloud DPU deployments).
